summary: Provision a brand-new Redshift data-warehouse cluster. No Redshift estate exists yet, so this is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new Redshift cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Redshift data warehouse; an engineer reviews the Terraform before it applies. Credentials are never entered here.
operations.description: Stand up a data warehouse as a complete specification: node type and count, network placement, and encryption. Credentials are never entered here — the engineer wires them through the secrets manager. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: cluster_name
params.label: Cluster identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the cluster endpoint.

params.name: node_type
params.label: Node type
params.help: The RA3 family separates compute from storage and is the AWS-recommended default for new clusters.

params.name: cluster_type
params.label: Cluster type
params.help: single-node runs exactly one node and ignores the node count below.

params.name: number_of_nodes
params.label: Number of nodes
params.help: Only applies to a multi-node cluster.

params.name: database_name
params.label: Initial database name
params.help: Leave blank for AWS's default database name (dev).

params.name: encrypted
params.label: Storage encryption
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the default AWS-managed key. Pick a key only for a customer-managed CMK.

params.name: automated_snapshot_retention_period
params.label: Keep automated snapshots (days)
params.help: Days of automated snapshots. 0 disables automated snapshots.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the cluster lives in. Empty until the estate provisions or imports a Redshift subnet group.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the cluster's port.

params.name: publicly_accessible
params.label: Public accessibility
params.help: Never public. This cluster is internal-only — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Cluster name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cluster holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cluster.

decisions: Master credentials go through the secrets manager — never entered in this portal
Parameter group and maintenance track are engineer decisions
IAM role attachments for Redshift Spectrum or COPY/UNLOAD to S3 are engineer follow-ups after creation
Elastic IP and enhanced VPC routing beyond the defaults are engineer decisions
